Healing or Harming? The Silent Crisis in Healthcare Sanitation

A UNICEF Data Report on Sanitation in Health Facilities Worldwide

Author

Ashish Vattam

Published

January 1, 2025

Code 
indicator2 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_2.csv")
metadata   = pl.read_csv("unicef_metadata.csv")

print(f"Indicator 2 shape  : {indicator2.shape}")
print(f"Metadata shape     : {metadata.shape}")
Indicator 2 shape  : (1822, 14)
Metadata shape     : (13715, 15)
Code 
print("=== Indicator 2 – obs_value summary ===")
print(indicator2.select("obs_value").describe())
print("\n=== Null counts ===")
print(indicator2.null_count())
n_countries = indicator2.select("country").n_unique()
year_min    = indicator2.select("time_period").min().item()
year_max    = indicator2.select("time_period").max().item()
print(f"\nCountries: {n_countries} | Years: {year_min}–{year_max}")

Countries: 119 | Years: 2000–2023
Code 
ind2_total = (
    indicator2
    .filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")
    .select(["country", "alpha_3_code", "time_period", "obs_value"])
)
country_avg = (
    ind2_total
    .group_by("country", "alpha_3_code")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("avg_no_sanitation"))
    .sort("avg_no_sanitation", descending=True)
)
meta_clean = (
    metadata
    .select(["country","alpha_3_code","year",
             "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)",
             "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)","Population, total"])
    .rename({"Life expectancy at birth, total (years)":"life_exp",
             "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)":"gdp_pc",
             "Population, total":"pop"})
    .filter(pl.col("year") >= 2015)
)
scatter_df = (
    ind2_total
    .filter(pl.col("time_period") >= 2015)
    .join(meta_clean, left_on=["alpha_3_code","time_period"], right_on=["alpha_3_code","year"], how="inner")
    .drop("country_right")
    .filter(pl.col("life_exp").is_not_null())
    .filter(pl.col("obs_value").is_not_null())
)
ts_global = (
    ind2_total
    .group_by("time_period")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("global_mean"))
    .sort("time_period")
)
country_avg_pd = country_avg.to_pandas()
scatter_pd     = scatter_df.to_pandas()
ts_pd          = ts_global.to_pandas()
top15          = country_avg_pd.head(15)
print("✓ All transforms complete")
✓ All transforms complete

Healing or Harming?

The Silent Crisis in Healthcare Sanitation

Ashish Vattam  ·  BAA1030 Data Analytics  ·  DCU Business School  ·  2025

119 countries covered

63.5% worst country (Kyrgyzstan)

2000–2023 years of data

0 meaningful improvements seen

The Crisis Nobody Talks About

When we think of hospitals and clinics, we imagine places of healing — sterile, safe, life-saving. But for hundreds of millions of people across the developing world, the reality is starkly different. The very facilities meant to protect life are operating without the most basic necessity: clean sanitation.

“A healthcare facility without proper sanitation is not a place of healing — it is a place of risk.” — UNICEF/WHO Joint Monitoring Programme

This report examines a deeply troubling UNICEF indicator — the percentage of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service. Using data from 119 countries spanning 2000 to 2023, we uncover a global crisis hiding in plain sight, one that disproportionately affects the world’s most vulnerable populations.

Why Sanitation in Healthcare Matters

Sanitation in healthcare facilities is not a luxury. It is a fundamental prerequisite for:

  • Infection prevention — absence of handwashing facilities allows pathogens to spread, particularly dangerous in post-surgical and maternity wards
  • Maternal and child safety — unsafe birth environments caused by poor sanitation contribute to neonatal infections and maternal mortality
  • Staff dignity and protocol adherence — healthcare workers cannot follow hygiene protocols without proper facilities
  • Public trust — communities that distrust facilities due to poor conditions avoid seeking care, creating a vicious cycle

The data reveals that in some countries, over 60% of health facilities lack any sanitation service. This is not a marginal problem — it is a systemic failure.

A World Divided: Global Sanitation Map

VISUALISATION 1 · WORLD MAP

Code 
SHAPEFILE = "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/pyogrio/tests/fixtures/naturalearth_lowres/naturalearth_lowres.shp"
world_gdf    = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE).to_crs("EPSG:4326")
world_merged = world_gdf.merge(country_avg_pd, left_on="iso_a3", right_on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

(
    ggplot(world_merged)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="avg_no_sanitation"), color="white", size=0.2)
    + scale_fill_gradientn(
        colors=["#eaf2f8","#aed6f1","#2e86c1","#1a5276","#0d2b45"],
        na_value="#d5d8dc", name="% of facilities\nwith no sanitation",
        limits=(0,75), breaks=[0,20,40,60,75],
        labels=["0%","20%","40%","60%","75%"]
    )
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(-180,180), ylim=(-55,85))
    + labs(
        title="Healthcare facilities with no sanitation service — global overview",
        subtitle="Average % across all available years  |  Grey = no data  |  Darker = more severe crisis",
        caption="Figure 1  ·  Source: UNICEF Indicator 2  ·  plotnine + GeoPandas"
    )
    + theme_void()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=15,weight="bold",color="#0d2b45",margin={"b":6}),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=11,color="#555555",margin={"b":10}),
        plot_caption=element_text(size=9,color="#888888"),
        legend_title=element_text(size=10,weight="bold",color="#1a5276"),
        legend_text=element_text(size=9,color="#333333"),
        legend_position="right",
        plot_background=element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8",color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8",color="#eaf2f8"),
        figure_size=(14,6)
    )
)

Key finding: Sub-Saharan Africa and South/Central Asia bear the heaviest burden. Europe and North America show near-zero rates — revealing a gap that is not just geographical, but fundamentally economic and political.

The Worst Offenders: Top 15 Countries

VISUALISATION 2 · BAR CHART

Code 
top15_sorted = top15.sort_values("avg_no_sanitation", ascending=True).copy()
top15_sorted["label"] = top15_sorted["avg_no_sanitation"].round(1).astype(str) + "%"
short_names = {"Tanzania, United Republic of":"Tanzania","Congo, the Democratic Republic of the":"DR Congo"}
top15_sorted["country"] = top15_sorted["country"].replace(short_names)
top15_sorted["country"] = pd.Categorical(top15_sorted["country"],categories=top15_sorted["country"].tolist(),ordered=True)

(
    ggplot(top15_sorted, aes(x="country", y="avg_no_sanitation", fill="avg_no_sanitation"))
    + geom_col(width=0.72)
    + geom_text(aes(label="label"), ha="left", nudge_y=0.4, size=9, color="#0d2b45", fontweight="bold")
    + scale_fill_gradientn(colors=["#aed6f1","#2e86c1","#1a5276","#0d2b45"],limits=(20,70),guide=None)
    + scale_y_continuous(limits=(0,82),breaks=[0,20,40,60,80],labels=["0%","20%","40%","60%","80%"])
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title="Top 15 countries: average % of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service",
        subtitle="Mean across all available years  |  Filtered to sex = 'Total'  |  Sorted descending",
        x="", y="Average % of facilities with no sanitation",
        caption="Figure 2  ·  geom_col() + coord_flip() + scale_fill_gradientn()"
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=13,weight="bold",color="#0d2b45",margin={"b":6}),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10,color="#555555",margin={"b":8}),
        plot_caption=element_text(size=9,color="#888888"),
        axis_text_y=element_text(size=11,color="#0d2b45"),
        axis_text_x=element_text(size=9,color="#555555"),
        axis_title_x=element_text(size=10,color="#0d2b45",margin={"t":8}),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_line(color="#dddddd",size=0.5),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_blank(),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8",color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size=(12,8)
    )
)

Kyrgyzstan leads with a staggering 63.5% — nearly two-thirds of its health facilities offer no sanitation. India’s position at 5th is especially alarming: even a modest percentage translates to hundreds of millions of people at risk.

The Life Expectancy Connection

VISUALISATION 3 · SCATTERPLOT WITH REGRESSION

Code 
scatter_agg = (
    scatter_pd.groupby("country")
    .agg(obs_value=("obs_value","mean"),life_exp=("life_exp","mean"))
    .reset_index().dropna(subset=["obs_value","life_exp"]).query("life_exp >= 50")
)
extremes = scatter_agg.nlargest(3,"obs_value")

(
    ggplot(scatter_agg, aes(x="obs_value", y="life_exp"))
    + geom_point(aes(color="life_exp"), size=3, alpha=0.75)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color=RED, fill=RED, alpha=0.15, size=1.2)
    + geom_text(data=extremes,mapping=aes(x="obs_value",y="life_exp",label="country"),
                size=8,color="#0d2b45",nudge_y=0.8,nudge_x=-1,ha="right")
    + scale_color_gradientn(colors=["#c0392b","#e67e22","#aed6f1","#1a5276","#0d2b45"],name="Life expectancy\n(years)")
    + scale_x_continuous(name="% of health facilities with no sanitation service",breaks=[0,10,20,30,40,50,60,70])
    + scale_y_continuous(name="Life expectancy at birth (years)",breaks=[50,55,60,65,70,75,80,85],limits=(50,87))
    + labs(
        title="Poor sanitation, shorter lives: the life expectancy connection",
        subtitle="Countries with more unsanitised health facilities tend to have lower life expectancy",
        caption="Figure 3  ·  geom_point() + geom_smooth(method='lm')  ·  UNICEF Indicator 2 + World Bank metadata"
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=13,weight="bold",color="#0d2b45",margin={"b":6}),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10,color="#555555",margin={"b":8}),
        plot_caption=element_text(size=9,color="#888888"),
        axis_text=element_text(size=9,color="#555555"),
        axis_title=element_text(size=10,color="#0d2b45"),
        legend_title=element_text(size=9,weight="bold",color="#0d2b45"),
        panel_grid_major=element_line(color="#eeeeee",size=0.4),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8",color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size=(12,7)
    )
)

The negative correlation is clear: as % of unsanitary facilities rises, life expectancy falls. Countries with near-zero unsanitary facilities consistently show life expectancies above 75 years, while countries above 50% fall below 65 years.

A Decade of Stagnation: 2000–2023

VISUALISATION 4 · TIME-SERIES

Code 
(
    ggplot(ts_pd, aes(x="time_period", y="global_mean"))
    + geom_line(color=TEAL, size=1.4)
    + geom_point(color=NAVY, size=2.5)
    + geom_hline(yintercept=ts_pd["global_mean"].mean(),linetype="dashed",color=RED,size=0.9,alpha=0.8)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=2020,linetype="dotted",color=AMBER,size=1,alpha=0.9)
    + annotate("text",x=2020.2,y=ts_pd["global_mean"].max()-0.2,label="COVID-19\n2020",size=8,color=AMBER,ha="left")
    + annotate("text",x=2001,y=ts_pd["global_mean"].mean()+0.2,label="Period average",size=8,color=RED,ha="left")
    + scale_x_continuous(name="Year",breaks=list(range(2000,2024,2)))
    + scale_y_continuous(name="Global average % (no sanitation)",breaks=[4,6,8,10,12])
    + labs(
        title="A decade of stagnation: global trends 2000–2023",
        subtitle="Global average % of healthcare facilities with no sanitation  |  No meaningful decline in 23 years",
        caption="Figure 4  ·  geom_line() + geom_point() + geom_hline() + geom_vline()  ·  119 countries"
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=13,weight="bold",color=NAVY,margin={"b":6}),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10,color="#555555",margin={"b":8}),
        plot_caption=element_text(size=9,color="#888888"),
        axis_text=element_text(size=9,color="#555555"),
        axis_title=element_text(size=10,color=NAVY),
        panel_grid_major=element_line(color="#eeeeee",size=0.4),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8",color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size=(13,6)
    )
)

The time-series reveals a deeply unsettling picture. Far from showing steady improvement, the global average has shown no sustained decline across more than two decades. This is not a problem in transition — it is a problem in stasis.

What Needs to Happen

The data makes one thing undeniable: healthcare sanitation is a global emergency that is not being treated as one. Three imperatives stand out:

  1. Targeted investment in the 15 worst-affected countries — especially Kyrgyzstan, Madagascar, India, and Nigeria
  2. Routine data collection must be strengthened in countries with data gaps
  3. Life expectancy targets cannot be met without addressing healthcare sanitation

The world cannot call itself committed to universal healthcare while millions of patients enter facilities that lack a working toilet. The data demands action.

About This Report

Detail Value
Indicator Proportion of health care facilities with no sanitation service
Source UNICEF Indicator 2 + World Bank Metadata
Countries covered 119
Time period 2000–2023
Tools Python · Polars · plotnine · GeoPandas · Quarto
Hosted on GitHub Pages

Report prepared as part of BAA1030 Data Analytics | DCU Business School
